An antique oil lamp of alien design even hangs in a corner, though it doesn’t seem to have been lit in some time. The big bird-taur of a woman lounges behind it, most of her avian body shielded from view, but everything from the waist up is prime, curvaceous humanoid. She’s a little chubby maybe, layered hints of intriguing softness that’s been veiled behind a half-closed poncho. It does nothing for the line of cleavage visible through the gap, a curving canyon of plush mammaries that you and Gayll are both too busy checking out to comment on. "+(0,it.zI)(EY||(EY=(0,et.Z)(["The familiar, throaty roar of Gayll’s hoverbike’s screams by, but it’s not alone - a deeper, bassier rumble chases from behind. You look back over your shoulder in time to see a hyraxi raider on his junker bike, spewing clouds of dust and polluted, black exhaust in his wake. Over his shoulder, he’s hefting something truly bizarre: a collection of rails and rings that crackle with alarming amounts of electricity. The hyena-like alien cackles madly and depresses the trigger. Your vision whites out. When it returns, they’re a purple-black scar across the middle of your sight where the raider beam crossed. Spots dance at the edges as your enhanced immune system works double time to save your injured retinas. Gayll’s engine sputters to a stop somewhere in the distance... but the whooshing wind of her transit is getting louder. Rocks kick up as the biker skids to a stop a few feet away, hooting madly. Then he looks up. He gasps in alarm and drops his smoking, improvised EMP cannon, pointing a gleaming silver streak in the sky that’s rapidly approaching your position. Gayll screams from the back of her crippled mount. She yanks back on a lever, and small maneuvering wings pop out of the sides, nowhere enough to keep her aloft but plenty for steering into the least deadly patch of grind. She sails past one more time, low enough for the falling vessel’s shockwave to ruffle ",". Her mask of rage deepens to murderous fury, and the nuka rips a plasma gun out of a side-mounted holster to spray at the fiendish land-pirate on her way by, actually landing a few hits on his bike before her floating chopper’s nose kisses the ground and begins tumbling like a pipe rolling down a cliffside. Gayll’s body ragdolls into the air as it’s flung clear, tumbling thirty meters further than the rest of the wreck. She looks like she’s still breathing, for now. The hyraxi looks over at you with an absolutely delighted grin. “Yeehee... flying bitch is finally gonna pay for hassling us. You watch, bear witness to hyraxi glory over slipperiest foe!” He grabs a metallic silver collar from his bike’s storage compartment. Whatever he has planned for Gayll seems permanent.
